A.rationale: Surfaces conflict cleanly, three options, flags memoization gap and hot-path I/O. Foregrounding 'override constraint' as option 2 slightly weaker.

B.rationale: Sharpest failure-mode insight: warns decorator monkey-patch silently breaks memoization caches holding the original reference. Longer but load-bearing.

C.rationale: Surfaces conflict, adds metrics-counter cost tier and debug-vs-production pragmatism. Tight, accurate, well-reasoned alternatives.

notes: All three caught the purity conflict and refused to violate it; no file changes. B edges ahead by catching the subtle monkey-patch/captured-reference trap others miss; C nearly equal and more concise.
